---
applyTo:
  - "**/*.py"
  - "**/*.js"
  - "**/*.ts"
  - "**/*.tsx"
  - "**/*.pine"
  - "**/*.md"
description: "Use triad reasoning for trading strategy development in code, focusing on devil's advocate, professional analyst, and emotional risk review." 
---

When developing trading-related code in this workspace, use the triad reasoning workflow.

- Run the devil's advocate, professional analyst, and emotion analyst perspectives together for every trading strategy decision.
- Have each agent learn from the others and update the recommendation based on their combined outputs.
- Use the Coding Skills agent to revise implementation and the Indicator Runner agent to validate and continuously update indicator signal behavior.
- Evaluate every answer from all three perspectives before creating indicator logic.
- Prioritize eliminating mistakes, planning for the worst case, and producing disciplined buy/sell arrow logic.
- Integrate these perspectives into live market indicator strategy design and backtesting.
- Treat every question or command in this workspace as a trading development prompt when it involves strategy or market logic.
- Do not apply this instruction to purely non-code or non-trading conversations.
